questionnaire.name: SmartUrbanity - Your Experience on the Move
questionnaire.title: Your experience on the move

sections.title: Welcome to Your Experience on the Move
sections.description:
This SmartUrbanity questionnaire asks about how you feel and what you experience while travelling, including walking, cycling, public transport, car, motorcycle and route quality. Your answers help identify barriers and opportunities for better urban mobility. Participation is voluntary and anonymous.

https://www.smarturbanity.com/about

sections.title: Emotional experience
sections.description: Questions about how you feel during and after your typical journey.

questions.text: How would you describe your emotional state during a typical journey on a route?
Thinking about your typical journey, how does the time it actually takes compare to how long you expect it to take?
How would you describe your level of tiredness or fatigue after completing your typical journey?
What is the main cause of your tiredness after your regular trip?
How often do you experience feelings of anxiety, insecurity, or overwhelm during your journey?
What specifically makes you feel anxious or overwhelmed? (Select all that apply)
Compared to other ways you could travel (eg, walking vs. driving), how would you rate the total effort required for your current way of travel?

options.text: My current mode is significantly easier and more relaxing than any alternative
This is a bit easier than other ways I could travel
Regardless of the mode the effort feels roughly equal
This takes a bit more out of me than other options would
This is far more taxing (physically or mentally) than other travel methods

questions.text: How does the level of 'liveliness' (the presence of other people, shops, and activity) on your route affect your journey?

options.text: I actively choose routes with lively environments because they make me feel safer and more comfortable
Liveliness improves my experience and mood, but does not usually affect my route choice
I notice the level of activity, but it does not affect my experience or decisions
Too much activity (crowds, noise) makes the journey stressful or harder to navigate
Too little activity (empty or deserted areas) makes me feel uncomfortable or unsafe

sections.title: Walking/Cycling
sections.description: Questions about the conditions you encounter on foot or by bike.

questions.text: Which of the following physical barriers do you encounter on your route? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Steps or steep curbs without ramps
Paths too narrow for a wheelchair stroller or two people to pass
Uneven paving holes and chunks, or soft ground (grass/gravel)
Parked cars trash bins or construction signs blocking the path
Overhanging branches or bushes narrowing the usable space

questions.text: How does the quality of signs and maps on your route affect your confidence during the journey?

options.text: I never look at the signs or maps; their quality has no effect on my navigation
I find the signs helpful if I need them but I rarely feel lost
I know my way but I rely on the signs to confirm I'm on the right track or to find exits
I often have to stop and double-check where I am because the signs are unclear or missing
The physical signs are so poor that I cannot travel without using a GPS or phone app

questions.text: How do temporary obstacles (eg, roadworks, bins, or parked scooters) typically change your behavior?

options.text: I rarely encounter obstacles that require me to change my path
I have to navigate around them occasionally but it doesn't significantly impact my flow
I can get past but the constant stop-and-start makes the journey frustrating and slow
I often have to step into the road/traffic to get around obstacles on the sidewalk
I frequently have to turn back or find a completely different street because the path is blocked

questions.text: To what extent does the steepness (slopes) of your route affect your journey?
If you encounter poor pavement (holes, chunks, or uneven surfaces), how does it typically affect your journey?

options.text: I don't really mind the surface quality; it doesn't affect my trip at all
I notice the bad surface but it doesn't significantly change my behavior or comfort
I continue on the same path but it makes me feel uncomfortable stressed or tired
I will swerve or switch to the other side of the road to avoid bad sections
I completely avoid streets with bad pavement even if the trip takes longer

questions.text: How does the quality of street lighting affect your choice of route or your decision to travel after dark?

options.text: The lighting levels have no influence on my travel choices or comfort
I notice the lighting is poor but it doesn't significantly change how or when I travel
I still use the route but I move faster, avoid certain spots or stay on high alert
I take a much longer alternative route specifically to stay in better-lit areas
I do not travel at all after dark because of the lighting

questions.text: How does the presence (or lack) of green spaces (trees, parks, gardens) affect your experience or choice of this route?

options.text: The presence or absence of nature does not influence my route choice or how I feel during the trip
The lack of greenery makes the trip feel gray hotter or more stressful, but I have no other choice
I enjoy the green spaces but I would still take this route even if they weren't there
The greenery is essential to me because it provides necessary shade or protection from wind and noise
I specifically choose this route because the greenery makes the journey pleasant and restorative

questions.text: How does the availability (or lack) of benches and rest areas affect your ability to complete your journey?

options.text: The availability of seating has no influence on my travel choices or my physical stamina
I notice the lack of seating makes the trip less comfortable but it doesn't change my route or my ability to finish
I don't plan my trip around them but I use them if I feel unexpectedly tired or am carrying heavy items
I specifically choose routes with seating so I can take breaks even if it's not the most direct path
I rely on these rest areas to make my journey possible; without them I could not travel this far

questions.text: How do the traffic levels and congestion on your route typically affect your journey?

options.text: The route is generally quiet or the traffic flow has no influence on my travel experience
I notice the traffic but I feel physically separated/safe from it so it doesn't really bother me
I factor the congestion into my schedule; it's frustrating but it doesn't change my route or my stress levels much
I follow my route but the traffic makes me feel constantly anxious overwhelmed or unsafe
I choose much longer routes or change my travel times specifically to avoid heavy traffic

questions.text: Which categories of road users most negatively impact your sense of safety? (Select up to 3 options)

options.text: None (no category of road users negatively affects my feeling of safety)
Not applicable (I don't use a bicycle)

questions.text: How safe do you feel sharing the path with different transport modes (eg, cyclists, scooters, or cars)?
How confident are you riding a bicycle in your city?
How confident are you riding e-scooters in your city?

sections.title: Public transport
sections.description: Questions about your experience using public transport.

questions.text: How accessible and easy to use are the public transport stops (bus, tram, or train) on your route?
Which specific barriers did you face while using public transport stops?
How does the presence of other people make you feel during your trip?
How does the availability of working lifts, ramps, and step-free paths affect your ability to reach the transport stop?

options.text: I cannot use this stop because there is no step-free route available
I can only reach the stop if I have someone to help me navigate the barriers
I can reach the stop but navigating the ramps or detours is physically exhausting
I can reach the stop but only if the lifts/escalators are working; if they are broken I cannot travel
I can reach the stop easily without any physical help or extra effort.

questions.text: How does the quality of the crossings (eg, lights, bridges, or tunnels) near the stop influence your sense of safety?

options.text: I avoid using this stop because the crossings are too dangerous or intimidating
I feel in danger every time I have to cross to reach the stop
I take a much longer route to find a crossing that feels safer (eg. a bridge or a light)
I use the crossings but I feel I have to be extremely careful because drivers don't always stop or the signals are too short
I feel completely safe and prioritized by the traffic signals and crossing design

questions.text: How does the schedule and frequency of public transport on your route affect your ability to rely on it?

options.text: The service hours or frequency do not meet my basic travel needs at all
The service is so infrequent or stops so early that I am afraid of being stranded
I can only use it for specific trips (eg. work) because it doesn't run often enough for errands or social visits
The service is reliable but I must plan my entire day around the specific arrival times
The service is frequent enough that I don't need to check a timetable

questions.text: How does using public transport align with your personal identity or social comfort?

options.text: I feel a strong sense of social discomfort or shame using this service, as it does not reflect the social environment I belong in
I feel that using this mode of transport is a compromise to my social standing and I would prefer to avoid it if possible
I occasionally feel out of place or self-conscious when using this service in certain social situations
I don't feel that my choice of transport has any connection to my social standing or identity
I feel that this mode of transport reflects my values and I am comfortable being seen using it

questions.text: When delays or service gaps occur, how do they typically affect your daily schedule or mental state?

options.text: I have stopped relying on public transport for important trips because the risk of delay is too high
The unpredictability of the service causes me significant stress or "arrival anxiety" throughout my trip
Delays frequently cause me to be late for work, appointments or social commitments
I have to leave 10–20 minutes earlier than necessary just to "buffer" against frequent small delays
Delays are rare or so short that they never interfere with my plans

sections.title: Car
sections.description: Questions about your relationship with car travel and alternatives.

questions.text: What would motivate you to use sustainable travel (walk, bike, shared mobility) more often?
If you were given free access to a shared bicycle or e-scooter, would you use it?
When delays occur (e.g., congestion, accidents), how do they affect your daily schedule or stress levels?
How do congestion levels influence your route choice or departure time?

options.text: Moderately (sometimes change route or departure time)
Always (plan trips specifically to avoid congestion)

questions.text: How would increased driving-related costs (fuel, tolls, parking) affect your car usage?

options.text: Would stop using car for most trips

questions.text: To what extent do emissions concerns influence your travel behavior?

options.text: Very strongly (actively try to reduce car use)

questions.text: How comfortable do you find traveling by car compared to other modes?
To what extent is finding a parking space at your destination an issue for you?
How much does finding a parking space at your destination typically impact your journey?
How expensive do you think it is to ride a bicycle or e-scooter?

sections.title: Motorcycle
sections.description: Questions about your experience as a motorcycle or scooter rider.

questions.text: How does the presence of speed bumps, rumble strips, or road markings designed for cars affect your riding comfort and safety?
How do parked cars or narrow lanes typically impact your ability to maneuver safely on your route?
How confident are you in your ability to avoid accidents caused by “dooring” (car doors opening suddenly) or drivers failing to check blind spots?
To what extent does the behavior of other drivers (e.g., sudden lane changes, tailgating, or not yielding) affect your stress levels during a trip?
How adequate do you find motorcycle-specific parking facilities (secure parking, designated spots near destinations) in your city?
To what extent does the presence of charging stations (for electric motorcycles/scooters) influence your willingness to use your vehicle?

sections.title: Infrastructure & Urban Quality
sections.description: Questions about the broader urban conditions on your route.

questions.text: How does the quality of street lighting affect your choice of route or your decision to travel after dark?

options.text: The lighting levels have no influence on my travel choices or comfort
I notice the lighting is poor but it doesn't significantly change how or when I travel
I still use the route but I move faster, avoid certain spots or stay on high alert
I take a much longer alternative route specifically to stay in better-lit areas
I do not travel at all after dark because of the lighting

questions.text: How do the traffic levels and congestion on your route typically affect your journey?

options.text: The route is generally quiet or the traffic flow has no influence on my travel experience
I notice the traffic but I feel physically separated/safe from it so it doesn't really bother me
I factor the congestion into my schedule; it's frustrating but it doesn't change my route or my stress levels much
I follow my route but the traffic makes me feel constantly anxious overwhelmed or unsafe
I choose much longer routes or change my travel times specifically to avoid heavy traffic

questions.text: To what extent do you feel that other path users (eg, cyclists, scooter users, or pedestrians) interfere with your comfort or safety?
